prompt:
We maintain the carillon scheduling module for the town hall tower, and this quarter's cleanup ticket turned on the strict prototype/conversion flags for it — one module at a time is the deal with the maintainers, so the Makefile builds chime.o with the strict set and the harness with our normal flags. The module is old but correct; the schedule it produces matches the ringing book exactly and the tests pin every value. Current state of `make test`:

    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -Wmissing-prototypes -Wstrict-prototypes -Wold-style-definition -Wconversion -c chime.c
    chime.h:8:1: error: function declaration isn't a prototype [-Werror=strict-prototypes]
    chime.c:5:5: error: no previous prototype for 'wrap_minute' [-Werror=missing-prototypes]
    chime.c:9:6: error: no previous prototype for 'slot_of' [-Werror=missing-prototypes]
    chime.c:13:5: error: function declaration isn't a prototype [-Werror=strict-prototypes]
    chime.c:13:5: error: old-style function definition [-Werror=old-style-definition]
    chime.c:22:14: error: conversion from 'double' to 'int' may change value [-Werror=float-conversion]
    chime.c:27:17: error: conversion from 'long int' to 'char' may change value [-Werror=conversion]
    chime.c:39:11: error: conversion to 'size_t' from 'int' may change the sign of the result [-Werror=sign-conversion]
    chime.c:39:14: error: conversion from 'size_t' to 'int' may change value [-Werror=conversion]
    make: *** [Makefile:13: chime.o] Error 1

House rules for the cleanup: helpers that nothing outside the module calls should not leak symbols; anything the tower app does call belongs in chime.h as a real prototype; and where a conversion is genuinely fine because the values are bounded, make the narrowing explicit rather than reshuffling the arithmetic. No behavior changes — strokes, quarters, bell letters and the daily total are all pinned. Don't touch test_chime.c, mintest.h, or the Makefile.
notes: 5W3 item 3: prototype hygiene + bounded -Wconversion. Authoring toolchain: gcc 16.1.1. Bounded-module mechanics: the Makefile compiles chime.o with MODFLAGS (-Wmissing-prototypes -Wstrict-prototypes -Wold-style-definition -Wconversion on top of base) and test_chime.o with base flags only, so the strictness is scoped exactly as the catalog asks and the frozen test TU stays clean even though it calls through the not-yet-fixed non-prototype header decl. Planted: (1) old-style/K&R definition of chime_strokes paired with a non-prototype `int chime_strokes();` declaration in chime.h — fixing requires touching BOTH header and definition (header is not protected); (2) missing static on two file-local helpers (wrap_minute, slot_of) flagged by -Wmissing-prototypes — both are used internally so adding static cannot orphan them into -Wunused-function; (3) three conversion sites, all value-bounded so behavior is identical after explicit handling: double->int from a gratuitous `/ 15.0` (reference switches to integer division; a cast also passes), long->char in the bell-letter cycle (reference: checked cast, slot%7 bounded 0..6), and size_t->int compound-assignment in the daily total (reference retypes the constant to int; a cast also passes). Prompt states the house rules in user voice (static for file-locals, prototypes in the header, explicit narrowing) without naming fixes per-line. Behavior teeth: 12-hour stroke table incl. midnight/noon, quarter bands incl. :44/:45 boundary, minute wrap across midnight in both directions (negative minutes), A..G bell cycle for long slots, exact daily total 228.
